steps.title: Rewrite both lines
steps.content: A system has no solution when the lines are parallel but distinct. Rewrite each equation in standard form: $$4x-6y=10y+2 \Rightarrow 4x-16y=2$$ $$ty=\frac{1}{2}+2x \Rightarrow -2x+ty=\frac{1}{2}$$

steps.title: Match the slopes
steps.content: Parallel lines have proportional $x$- and $y$-coefficients. Compare the second equation to the first: the $x$-coefficient changed from $4$ to $-2$, so the scale factor is $-\frac{1}{2}$. Apply that to the $y$-coefficient: $$-16\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)=8$$ Thus $t=8$.

steps.title: Confirm no solution
steps.content: When $t=8$, the two lines have the same slope but different constants, so they are distinct parallel lines. They never intersect, so the system has no solution. Enter 8.
